frameworks.name: resource-rational exploration
frameworks.one_line: Exploration policy shaped by cognitive capacity constraints on uncertainty handling.

frameworks.name: uncertainty-directed exploration
frameworks.one_line: Approach-uncertainty heuristic extended to include uncertainty avoidance regime.

frameworks.name: policy comparison modeling
frameworks.one_line: Human choices benchmarked against a range of plausible exploration policies.

core_question: How do humans reconcile conflicting tendencies to approach versus avoid uncertainty during exploration?
key_assumptions: Exploration serves to reduce goal-relevant uncertainty
Cognitive capacity for processing uncertainty is limited
Choice between better-known and lesser-known options indexes exploratory policy
manipulations: Overall uncertainty level about choice options across trials
Relative knownness of the two options (better-known vs lesser-known)
key_findings: Participants generally chose to approach uncertainty
When overall uncertainty was highest, participants instead avoided uncertainty and sampled better-known options
Uncertainty-avoidance was associated with faster decisions
Avoidance did not impair learning despite reducing observed information rate
interpretations: Balancing approach and avoidance of uncertainty reduces cognitive costs of exploration in a resource-rational manner
The approach/avoid balance reflects capacity constraints rather than a single fixed exploration heuristic
